steps.title: Identify the antecedent's number
steps.content: The passage refers to "his famous phrase" — a single, singular phrase. The blank must use a singular possessive noun for "phrase" and a singular pronoun to refer back to it.

steps.title: Apply correct singular forms
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: possessive noun form and pronoun-antecedent agreement. Rule: singular nouns form the possessive with apostrophe-s (phrase's), and the pronoun referring to a singular noun must also be singular (its).
  • Choice A — "phrases' simplicity masks its": "phrases'" is a plural possessive, but there is only one phrase. Wrong number on the noun. ✗
  • Choice B — "phrases simplicity masks their": "phrases" has no possessive apostrophe (not a possessive at all), and "their" is plural — both are wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — "phrase's simplicity masks their": "phrase's" is correctly singular possessive, but "their" is a plural pronoun that doesn't match the singular "phrase." ✗
  • Choice D — "phrase's simplicity masks its": "phrase's" is singular possessive and "its" is a singular pronoun — both agree with the one phrase. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
